verses.text: 'You only have I known of all the families of the earth: therefore I will punish you.' Privilege and peril. Closeness to God brings greater accountability, not exemption.
'You only have I known of all the families of the earth: therefore I will punish you.' Privilege and peril. Closeness to God brings greater accountability, not exemption.
Can two walk together unless agreed? A lion roars over prey; 'shall there be evil in a city, and the LORD hath not done it?' Cause and effect. Nothing befalls a people without God's hand behind it.
Can two walk together unless agreed? A lion roars over prey; 'shall there be evil in a city, and the LORD hath not done it?' Cause and effect. Nothing befalls a people without God's hand behind it.
Can two walk together unless agreed? A lion roars over prey; 'shall there be evil in a city, and the LORD hath not done it?' Cause and effect. Nothing befalls a people without God's hand behind it.
Can two walk together unless agreed? A lion roars over prey; 'shall there be evil in a city, and the LORD hath not done it?' Cause and effect. Nothing befalls a people without God's hand behind it.
'Surely the Lord GOD will do nothing, but he revealeth his secret unto... the prophets'; the lion has roared, who will not fear? Forewarning. God warns through His prophets before He acts.
'Surely the Lord GOD will do nothing, but he revealeth his secret unto... the prophets'; the lion has roared, who will not fear? Forewarning. God warns through His prophets before He acts.
Let the heathen behold the tumults in Samaria; they 'know not to do right,' storing up violence. Lost conscience. A people steeped in wrong forgets how to do right.
Let the heathen behold the tumults in Samaria; they 'know not to do right,' storing up violence. Lost conscience. A people steeped in wrong forgets how to do right.
An adversary shall come; as a shepherd rescues 'two legs' from the lion, so shall a scrap of Israel be saved. Bare remnant. Judgment will leave only fragments of the proud nation.
An adversary shall come; as a shepherd rescues 'two legs' from the lion, so shall a scrap of Israel be saved. Bare remnant. Judgment will leave only fragments of the proud nation.
He will visit the altars of Bethel and smite the houses of ivory; the great houses shall perish. Luxury toppled. Idolatry and opulence fall together under His hand.
